channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Violence Surges in Culiacán Amid Cartel Power Struggles After El Mencho's Death
item.description: Culiacán, a city in Sinaloa, Mexico, has become a focal point of extreme violence following the capture and death of Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as 'El Mencho', leader of the Jalisco cartel. The...

item.title: Thomas Morgan sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering five-month-old Jensen-Lee Dougal
item.description: Thomas Morgan, 29, from Gorseinon in Swansea, has been sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of his girlfriend’s five-month-old son, Jensen-Lee Dougal. The sentence was handed down at Swansea ...

item.title: Cuban Forces Kill Four on US-Registered Speedboat in Territorial Waters Clash
item.description: The Cuban government has reported that its forces shot and killed four individuals aboard a US-registered speedboat that entered Cuban waters and opened fire on a patrol vessel. Six others on the spee...

item.title: Keir Starmer Pledges Review of England's Student Loan System Amid Calls for Reform
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer, leader of the UK Labour Party, has pledged to explore ways to make the student loan system in England fairer. His comments came during a parliamentary session, following criticism fr...

item.title: Block reduces workforce by almost half to prioritise artificial intelligence initiatives
item.description: Jack Dorsey, co-founder of Twitter, has cut nearly 40% of the workforce at his fintech company Block, which owns Cash App, reducing the number of employees from over 10,000 to just under 6,000. Dorsey...

item.title: Live Coverage: Nottingham Forest and Fenerbahce Clash in Europa League
item.description: Access to the Telegraph article regarding the Europa League match between Nottingham Forest and Fenerbahce is currently restricted. Visitors attempting to view the live score are met with an 'Access D...

item.title: The Bayeux Tapestry Loan to the British Museum: Cultural Significance and Risks
item.description: Later this year, the Bayeux Tapestry, an 11th-century masterpiece and one of Europe's earliest visual narratives, will be loaned from France to the British Museum for a major exhibition running from S...

item.title: EU Anti-Fraud Office to review Mandelson's actions as trade commissioner amid Epstein allegations
item.description: Peter Mandelson, former EU Trade Commissioner and UK government minister, is under scrutiny by the European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF) following concerns about his conduct during his tenure in Brussels....

item.title: European Parliament President Calls for Stronger EU Action Against Russian Shadow Fleet
item.description: The European Parliament President, Roberta Metsola, has criticized the European Union for not taking sufficient measures to stop Russia's 'shadow fleet' from transporting sanctioned oil. Speaking to S...

item.title: Zelensky opens up about fatherhood struggles amid Russia's invasion of Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has admitted the toll the ongoing war with Russia has had on his role as a father, acknowledging that he is not the best parent due to the overwhelming responsib...

item.title: Emails reveal model scout Daniel Siad’s correspondence with Jeffrey Epstein regarding young women
item.description: Newly declassified emails reveal the extensive correspondence between French-born, Swedish citizen model scout Daniel Siad and financier Jeffrey Epstein. Spanning over a decade, these communications d...

item.title: Study Links Higher Cancer Mortality Rates to Proximity of Nuclear Power Plants in the U.S.
item.description: A study published in February 2026 reveals that counties in the U.S. located closer to operational nuclear power plants have higher cancer mortality rates compared to those farther away. The study, wh...

item.title: BBC investigates incident involving racial slur during BAFTAs broadcast
item.description: The BBC has launched a fast-tracked investigation after a racial slur was unintentionally broadcast during the BAFTAs ceremony. The offensive remark was shouted by John Davidson, a campaigner with Tou...

item.title: Analysis of the Gorton and Denton By-Election and Its Political Implications
item.description: The upcoming Gorton and Denton by-election in Greater Manchester has attracted significant political attention, partly due to speculation over the return of Andy Burnham to Westminster. Although Burnh...

item.title: Guide to the Top Prams, Pushchairs and Buggies for Children
item.description: The original Telegraph article aimed to provide parents with a comprehensive guide to the best prams, pushchairs and buggies available on the market. Although the page is currently inaccessible due to...

item.title: Guide to Choosing Comfortable Outdoor Changing Robes
item.description: The Telegraph's article aimed to provide a guide on the best changing robes for outdoor use. Unfortunately, the content is currently inaccessible, showing an 'Access Denied' message when attempting to...

item.title: Labour faces tight contest in Gorton and Denton by-election amid challenge from Reform UK and the Greens
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is facing a tense wait as votes are counted in the Gorton and Denton by-election, a contest widely seen as a critical test of his leadership. Labour won the Greater Manchester seat co...

item.title: Pakistan launches airstrikes on Kabul and other Afghan provinces in response to cross-border attack by Afghan forces
item.description: Tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan have escalated sharply following a series of retaliatory military actions along their disputed border, known as the Durand Line. On Thursday night, Afghan for...

item.title: Interim Report Highlights Structural Issues in England's Maternity Services
item.description: An interim government-commissioned review led by Baroness Amos has identified serious shortcomings in maternity services across England. The report highlights six key issues, including staffing shorta...

item.title: Champions League Last 16: Key Matches and Predictions for the Quarter-Final Spots
item.description: The Champions League last 16 stage promises to be thrilling, with Arsenal and Liverpool looking confident in their chances of reaching the quarter-finals, while Manchester City and Newcastle face more...
